title: Brunia angulata
taxonomy: Brunia angulata is classified within the family Bruniaceae, which is a family of flowering plants in the order Bruniales. This family is endemic to the Cape Provinces of South Africa and contains around 18 species. Brunia angulata is a member of the genus Brunia, which consists of about 50 species of shrubs and small trees. The Brunia angulata species is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. It is a succulent plant that is adapted to the arid and semi-arid conditions of its native habitat.

properties: Brunia angulata, also known as the prickly pear cactus, has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds and burns due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
